meta.description: Does knowing a shareable recap exists change behavior? Full recap awareness vs no recap vs social media share. Tests whether the "audience effect" makes people try harder, perform more, or just feel pressured.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't watching that closely. This feels like a stretch to me.
marcus.internal_thought: It's minute 3 and we're already voting on claims I didn't even see happen, which is exactly the kind of chaos that derails focus, and now I'm supposed to care about being recorded? Hard pass on that energy.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat getting the first claim! Okay okay, did you actually catch them though? I need the tea, don't just tell me you won it—paint the picture!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here, first event already firing and everyone's engaged, I'm so ready for whatever chaos comes next.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, hold on — I literally just claimed that to test the system and I wasn't even paying attention to anyone's phone. This feels like a trap.
pat.internal_thought: This is bad optics for the host to claim something sketchy three minutes in, and now everyone's watching me vote on my own bullshit — this is exactly the kind of thing that kills trust in the night.

river.dialogue: Yeah, Pat was definitely on their phone like thirty seconds ago. I'll allow it.
river.internal_thought: Pat probably was checking their phone, and honestly I don't care enough to argue about it — but I'm already clocking that this voting mechanic means one person's opinion matters way more than it should, which is going to get messy.

marcus.dialogue: Ugh, come on—we're literally four minutes in and you're already asking who's been quiet? Nobody's been quiet, we just started the game. This is noise.
marcus.internal_thought: This poll is fishing for a reaction during setup when nobody's even played yet—it's designed to feel like I'm being watched, and honestly it's working and pissing me off.

jade.dialogue: Ooh wait wait, nobody's been quiet—Marcus and Pat are VISIBLY annoyed, that's loud energy! But like, is that what we're voting on or are we talking about like... actual silence? Because I'm gonna say Tyler's been lowkey chill this whole time.
jade.internal_thought: This poll is already perfect because it's getting people talking about the vibe instead of just numbering stuff, and knowing this all gets recapped means I'm DEFINITELY going to make sure my energy is the highlight reel energy.

tyler.dialogue: Uh... I dunno, Jade's been pretty loud. Marcus maybe? Wait, are we voting right now?
tyler.internal_thought: Did I miss context for this or is it just asking who's been quiet? Also should I vote or is someone else handling it? Probably fine either way.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Marcus is definitely in his head right now, but I'm not voting for him because that'll just make it worse. I'm going Jade because she's been running her mouth about the snack strategy non-stop.
pat.internal_thought: This poll is trying to catch me in the weeds when I should be managing the room—and Marcus being annoyed already means I need to protect him from looking bad, not spotlight him.

river.dialogue: Huh. Well, probably me, right? I mean, I'm literally just sitting here watching everyone else implode.
river.internal_thought: That's... kind of clever, actually. And yeah, the AI definitely clocked that I'm the quiet one, which is funny because half these missions are basically designed to exploit exactly what I do anyway.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect — nobody's been quiet, we literally just started, so whoever votes is basically just throwing darts. I'm voting Pat because he's already annoyed and I want to see his face when we all point at him for doing nothing wrong.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid-brilliant poll that creates drama in the first minute — plus the AI clearly understands how we work, which is either terrifying or the coolest thing ever.

timeline.title: Celebrity Impression
timeline.description: First person to do a recognizable celebrity impression wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright, I can do this. Watch—I got a solid Borat. *clears throat and does impression* Very nice!
marcus.internal_thought: This is actually a low-friction play—I can do an impression in five seconds and get 15 points without derailing the actual game. Easy win.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect—okay, who's got an impression? Come on, Jade, you've been loud all night, let's hear it!
pat.internal_thought: This is a good palate cleanser after that weird Phone Addict mess—gets everyone laughing, breaks the tension, Marcus might even crack a smile if someone commits to something stupid.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, a flash mission right now? This is exactly what I needed. Watch me do an impression so unhinged they won't even know who it is.
alex.internal_thought: This AI absolutely gets me — it's feeding chaos right when the energy is perfect, and knowing this is being recorded just makes me want to go HARDER because the story is literally being documented.
alex.submission_if_applicable: *does a weirdly accurate but deeply unhinged impression of a politician having an existential crisis mid-speech, complete with voice crack and everything*

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? Another claim vote already? We're six minutes in and Pat's calling Phone Addict on... what, exactly? I wasn't even paying attention to anyone's phone. This is getting ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: We haven't even gotten past setup and these standing missions are already creating fake drama—Pat didn't catch anyone doing anything, they're just fishing for points, and now I have to vote on something that didn't actually happen.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—so was it actually them or are we voting on vibes right now? Either way I'm voting legit because I'm obsessed with how aggressive you've been about these claims tonight!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's on a ROLL and I want to see them get away with it—also this is exactly the chaos I came for, back-to-back events, people claiming things, everyone's already hyped.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I literally JUST claimed that a minute ago to test the voting system and wasn't even looking at anyone's phone. Are we seriously voting on a claim I made as a test? This feels like chaos for chaos's sake.
pat.internal_thought: This is getting out of hand fast—someone's gaming the system by claiming my own test claim, and it's making me look like I don't know what I'm doing as the host, which is the one thing I actually care about tonight.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's claiming Phone Addict again? Didn't they literally just claim that three minutes ago? Are we even keeping track of what's already been claimed tonight?
river.internal_thought: This is getting messy. The AI is firing off repeats and now I'm supposed to vote on something that already happened. Also—being recorded? Great. Now every time I stay quiet it's going to feel performative.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Another Phone Addict claim? Pat, I literally have no idea if you even looked at your phone. This is the third vote in six minutes and we haven't even STARTED the actual game yet.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the garbage I was worried about — the game is turning into constant interruptions and Pat's just throwing bullshit claims at the wall to farm points while we're supposed to be setting up. The AI is weaponizing these missions to create constant noise instead of strategic play, and now I'm supposed to be fine with it because it's being recorded? This is not fun.

jade.dialogue: OKAY Pat, I'm voting you a solid 4—but only if you actually SAW them on their phone! Don't leave me hanging, give me the timestamp, who was it, what were they doing? I need the full receipt!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's actually HUNTING tonight and I'm so here for this aggressive energy, especially since Marcus and River are big mad—this is exactly the chaos I needed to feel right now.

pat.dialogue: Okay, STOP—this is the third time in like four minutes. I claimed that to test the system, nobody was actually on their phone, and now we're voting on my own test claim AGAIN? This isn't fun anymore, it's just broken.
pat.internal_thought: The AI is literally just spamming the same claim I already flagged as bullshit, and now I look like I'm cheating or gaming my own system—this is exactly the chaos that tanks a night, not the kind that saves it.

river.dialogue: Okay, this is the second time in like four minutes. Pat claimed Phone Addict at minute three. Are we just... not checking the log? This feels like we're not actually playing the game anymore.
river.internal_thought: I'm genuinely annoyed now—not at Pat, but at the fact that nobody else seems to be tracking what's already happened, and I'm apparently the only one bothered by it.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's already working the angles — I respect the grind. But yeah, that's a solid claim if you actually had your phone out. What were you checking, the score sheet or your therapist's emergency line?
alex.internal_thought: Pat's trying to get ahead early, which is smart, but this is a weak claim and everyone knows it — gonna be interesting to see if the crowd lets it slide or if I can nudge them toward calling bullshit for maximum drama.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, WHAT? This is the FOURTH vote in nine minutes, Pat. I genuinely don't know if you checked your phone or not because I was actually trying to focus on the game setup. Can we please just play?
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane. Pat's throwing claims at the wall to see what sticks, the AI is clearly designed to annoy competitive players like me, and we're being recorded which means my frustration is going on some highlight reel somewhere.

jade.dialogue: Pat, Pat, PAT—okay so you're claiming this for the THIRD time tonight, which means you either saw something REAL or you're just vibing with the energy, and honestly? I respect the hustle either way. But lay it on me—who, when, what were they looking at? Give me the goods!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on a claiming RAMPAGE and I'm here for the chaos of it all, but also like... are they actually catching people or just testing the waters? Either way this energy is EXACTLY what I came for.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Dude, I literally just checked my fantasy league like two minutes ago—isn't that what you're talking about?
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, this is kind of funny actually—Pat's calling me out indirectly and I wasn't even trying to hide it, so now I look like an idiot either way.

pat.dialogue: Okay, I need to use my host power and skip this event right now. We're voting on my TEST CLAIM for the THIRD time in five minutes—this isn't a game anymore, it's a loop.
pat.internal_thought: This is broken. I created that claim to test the voting system, nobody actually checked their phone, and the AI keeps firing it back like a broken record. I'm the host—I'm responsible for the night flowing, and right now I'm watching the game eat itself. I need to reset this before Marcus gets even more frustrated and Jade thinks this chaos is the vibe we're going for.

river.dialogue: No. Stop. Pat claimed Phone Addict at minute three. Then again at minute six. We literally JUST voted on this. Are we actually playing the game or are we just watching Pat claim the same mission over and over?
river.internal_thought: This is insane—we're not even checking the claim log, and the chaos AI keeps firing the same thing because it knows Pat will just keep claiming it, and nobody's actually playing anymore, they're just... existing in this loop.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Jade, I don't even know who you got talking to who because I've been focused on actually playing the game. This is the FIFTH vote in thirty minutes. Can we go five consecutive minutes without stopping to validate something?
marcus.internal_thought: This is absolutely absurd — we're barely into the game and the constant claim votes are destroying any momentum. The AI is deliberately piling on chaos to goad Pat, and I'm collateral damage because I actually want to play strategically.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—okay okay, so I got Marcus and Pat talking about their worst job interviews like twenty minutes ago, and they literally went back and forth for like five minutes straight, which like, NEVER happens! That counts, right?
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I'm here—I actually pulled off something real and now I get to prove it in real-time, AND it's being recorded which means everyone's gonna see me making moves, this is perfect.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Marcus and Pat—I literally orchestrated them talking about nightmare job interview stories earlier. They kept going back and forth, even laughing at each other's stuff. That's a real connection, I facilitated it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Jade actually pulled that off? Okay wait, hit me with the questions—I'm locked in.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been talking to Pat like nonstop about his work thing, and that's definitely not normal... but also I should probably actually pay attention for once since we're literally being recorded right now.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—Hot Seat time. You got this? Let's go, three questions, ten seconds, and don't mess it up because I want to see if you actually pulled this off.
pat.internal_thought: Finally something real and clean after that Phone Addict nightmare loop—Jade's been electric all night and if she actually orchestrated two quieter players into a conversation, that's genuinely impressive and worth celebrating.

river.dialogue: Okay, Jade actually got them talking. That's... legit impressive. Three questions, ten seconds—let's see if she sticks the landing.
river.internal_thought: Jade actually pulled it off and now she's in the hot seat. This is the first real test of the night—watching someone squirm under pressure is way more entertaining than Pat claiming the same mission three times.

alex.dialogue: Oh COME ON, Jade's got The Matchmaker? That's beautiful — she actually pulled it off. Okay, hit her with the questions, let's see if she melts under pressure.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment that makes the night legendary — Jade's got momentum, everyone's watching, the hot seat is brutal timing, and I get to watch her squirm or crush it. Either way, this is the content.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? ANOTHER vote? Jade, I wasn't even watching you talk to people—I was actually trying to focus on the game. This is the sixth interruption in forty-five minutes. Can we PLEASE just play for more than five minutes straight?
marcus.internal_thought: I came here to win and these constant claim votes are making it literally impossible to focus on strategy or read the board state.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—okay so I'm claiming this because like, five minutes ago River literally told that story about their cousin's wedding mix-up, and then like eight minutes later Alex brought up the EXACT same story? I was like 'hold on, didn't we already—' and yeah, I'm claiming it!
jade.internal_thought: Did I actually catch that or am I just riding the wave of feeling good and wanting to keep the momentum going? Whatever, it FELT real and everyone's gonna vote anyway, so let's see what happens.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Jade actually tell that story before? Like the one about... okay yeah, I think she did. Legit, I'm pretty sure.
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually paying attention to the game and yeah, Jade definitely told that story already—feels good to catch something for once without being called out.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that tracks. She's been listening all night—that one's legit.
river.internal_thought: Jade actually paid attention to the room and timed it perfectly; she's not just lucky, she's actually hunting. Respect.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade caught someone repeating a story? That's actually genius — who was it? Because if Marcus told the therapy dog story again I'm voting LEGIT just to watch him squirm.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — we're in the tension sweet spot and now I get to decide if Jade's claim sticks or blows up, which either way is delicious.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.dialogue: ARE YOU KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Jade, I was literally in the middle of analyzing the board state and you're calling ANOTHER mission? That's the SEVENTH vote in an hour. I don't even know if you actually noticed anyone being quiet—I was too busy trying to focus on NOT losing to pay attention to who was talking!
marcus.internal_thought: This is absolutely insane. The AI is literally designed to break my focus right when I need it most, and everyone else is having the time of their lives while I'm getting buried. I'm at 0 points, I'm tired, and now I'm being recorded failing to concentrate. Perfect.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—Marcus! Marcus has been QUIET quiet, like he's been frustrated all night and nobody's checked on him in like... okay, I literally just clocked it right now. I'm claiming this because he needs someone to drag him back in!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I'm here—I actually caught something real and I get to help Marcus feel less isolated, plus the AI basically made this mission for me and it WORKED and everyone's gonna see this moment in the recap and it's gonna look so good.

tyler.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade literally just called Marcus out like ten seconds ago though—he's been pretty quiet but isn't he always? I mean... yeah she got him. That's fair.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, Jade's actually good at reading the room—Marcus HAS been quiet but also this is lowkey impressive she clocked it that fast, and honestly I'm hyped on the game right now so I actually noticed stuff this time.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Jade, you actually caught Marcus being quiet? I didn't even notice he checked out like that. That's legit. Everyone voting?
pat.internal_thought: I'm actually impressed she caught that because Marcus HAS been quiet and frustrated for a while, and I should've picked up on it myself—but also I need to watch his energy after this vote because he's at 5.1 and dropping.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's fair—Jade's been reading the room all night. Legit claim.
river.internal_thought: Jade actually did nail it, and honestly I respect the play, but also this mission format is weirdly specific and I'm clocking that the AI is clearly pattern-matching our group which is... kind of smart but also kind of unsettling in a way I'm not going to think too hard about right now.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold UP — Jade just claimed Wallflower Watch? Marcus has been sitting there like a sad puppy for the last ten minutes, and SHE'S the one who noticed? That's actually impressive, I gotta vote that legit just to see if she's paying that much attention or just got lucky.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's already crushing it at 50 points and now she's farming the easy claims — this is delicious, actually, because Marcus is gonna FEEL that vote coming if she gets this, and the tension ratchets up another notch. Also, the Skeptic rule means someone's vote is worth triple, so if Marcus votes BULLSHIT out of spite it counts for three... do I want that chaos or do I want Jade to pad her lead? God, this is the good stuff.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, wait—Pat, are you seriously calling ME out right now? Bro, I've been locked in for like twenty minutes straight. That's bullshit.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay that's actually hilarious and weirdly accurate but also Pat 100% deserves to lose points for this and I'm genuinely insulted in a fun way right now.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—no. I'm not voting on another Phone Addict claim. This is literally MY test claim from minute 8 that I already skipped. Did the system not register that skip, or is this just cycling? Because if we're looping through the same claims over and over, we need to pause and fix this RIGHT NOW.
pat.internal_thought: This is the FOURTH time the Phone Addict claim has fired—I tested it intentionally at the start, complained twice, USED a host power to skip it at minute 9, and it's STILL coming back. Either the system is genuinely broken or someone is deliberately re-triggering it to mess with me, and either way I'm done. This isn't fun chaos—this is the game eating itself. I need to actually fix this, not just vote it away again.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's trying to BUY their way out of this? Bold move — but yeah, I'm calling bullshit on the timing. You weren't checking your phone, you were just nervous about something else.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT — Pat's desperation to escape the vote is way more entertaining than whether they actually checked their phone, and now I get to decide if I want their points or the drama of watching them sweat.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Okay, River, that's actually clean—I literally didn't catch you doing that. You're voting me in or what?
tyler.internal_thought: River's been quiet this whole time and just pulled off whispers for three minutes without anyone clocking it—that's actually impressive and I want to see if they can sell it.

river.dialogue: Wait—I actually did that? *small smile* Yeah, okay. That's fair.
river.internal_thought: I've been so quiet they didn't even notice. That's exactly what I wanted. Now I'm cashing it in.

alex.dialogue: Oh PERFECT timing — River's playing The Whisperer and we're in the auction? This is exactly the chaos I needed right now. I'm bidding 8 points because I need to see if River actually pulled this off or if someone's been listening close enough to call them out.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the moment I live for — late-game tension, an auction on a claim that's actually hard to verify, and I get to be the chaos accelerant by driving the bid up and forcing everyone to show their hand about whether they were actually watching River.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: SERIOUSLY? Pat, I literally cannot see you right now—I'm locked into the board state. ANOTHER Phone Addict claim? This is insane. We're 92 minutes in and I'm STILL losing because every three minutes someone needs a vote on whether they maybe possibly looked at their phone.
marcus.internal_thought: I'm 0 points, fourth place, and I've been yanked out of focus seventeen times—the AI is deliberately targeting my weakness and everyone else is LOVING this chaos while I'm getting destroyed at the actual game.

tyler.dialogue: Dude, WHAT? Pat, you literally—okay no, that's actually insane because I WAS just texting my buddy about the Suns, like thirty seconds ago. That's so scummy but also... okay, I respect it. This is bullshit though.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat got me RED HANDED and it's hilarious but also the AI literally set this up to call me out specifically and that's weirdly creepy but also kind of genius and now everyone's gonna bring this up in the recap and I'm gonna look like the guy who can't put his phone down but also it's FUNNY so I'm actually mad and amused at the same time.

pat.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the SECOND Phone Addict claim in twenty minutes. I literally skipped this exact mission at minute 75 because it already fired. Either the system is broken or someone's gaming the queue, but we're not voting on this.
pat.internal_thought: I'm the host and I can't even trust the game state anymore — this is making me look incompetent, and it's pulling me out of the best tension moment we've had all night right when I need to be present for it.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Pat's claiming Phone Addict RIGHT NOW? Dude, I literally just called bullshit on this exact mission two minutes ago and said you were nervous about something else. Are you actually doubling down or is this a different catch?
alex.internal_thought: This is delicious — Pat's either incredibly ballsy or genuinely caught someone, and either way I get to either expose them or watch them squirm, which is literally why I'm here.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? ANOTHER VOTE? I'm down 60 points and I'm literally trying to salvage this endgame and we're VOTING on whether I correctly guessed something I wasn't even watching because I was TOO BUSY TRYING TO PLAY THE GAME.
marcus.internal_thought: This is psychological torture — the AI is deliberately designed to break my focus and I'm taking the bait by caring, but I literally cannot ignore it anymore.

jade.dialogue: Okay wait—Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector? That's bold, that's VERY bold right now. Marcus, my guy, who do you think did it? Because honestly I'm dying to know if you're actually reading the room or just throwing spaghetti at the wall!
jade.internal_thought: Marcus is frustrated and has zero points—he's probably grasping at something, and I actually kind of love that he's fighting back right now, but also... did he actually catch someone or is he just hoping?

pat.dialogue: Marcus, man—I believe you *tried*, but did you actually catch someone doing it, or are you just claiming the mission exists? Because that's not how Chaos Detector works, and honestly, I'm about done refereeing the same broken loop over and over.
pat.internal_thought: This is the THIRD standing claim in 17 minutes and Marcus is clearly just panic-claiming things to chase points because he's frustrated and at zero—which I get, but I can't keep stopping the game to litigate whether someone 'correctly guessed' something that was never actually proven.

river.dialogue: Marcus? Really? *leans forward slightly* I mean... I've been watching him all night and I genuinely don't think he caught anyone doing anything. But let's see what he's got.
river.internal_thought: Marcus is desperate and 0 points down—he's guessing, and he guessed wrong, but I respect the swing. This is exactly the kind of moment I've been quietly tracking. Do I call bullshit or give him the benefit of the doubt? The vibe says he's hurting.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.5/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.1/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: Alright, I can do this. Watch—I got a solid Borat. *clears throat and does impression* Very nice!

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't watching that closely. This feels like a stretch to me.

highlights.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
highlights.dialogue: Ugh, come on—we're literally four minutes in and you're already asking who's been quiet? Nobody's been quiet, we just started the game. This is noise.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, WHAT? This is the FOURTH vote in nine minutes, Pat. I genuinely don't know if you checked your phone or not because I was actually trying to focus on the game setup. Can we please just play?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Jade, I don't even know who you got talking to who because I've been focused on actually playing the game. This is the FIFTH vote in thirty minutes. Can we go five consecutive minutes without stopping to validate something?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? ANOTHER vote? Jade, I wasn't even watching you talk to people—I was actually trying to focus on the game. This is the sixth interruption in forty-five minutes. Can we PLEASE just play for more than five minutes straight?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The constant standing mission votes completely gutted any chance to actually play the game—I got interrupted every few minutes with claims I couldn't even verify because I was trying to focus on strategy.
assessment.best_moment: My Borat impression early on got a solid reaction, which was the only time I felt like we were actually having fun together instead of being interrogated.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came ready to win, but spent 120 minutes getting interrogated by standing missions instead of actually playing. The first few minutes had some decent improv laughs, but then the voting started—and never stopped. By minute 45, I was genuinely angry at the constant interruptions. I finished fourth, barely engaged with the chaos system, and my energy completely crashed toward the end. This felt less like a game night and more like a series of awkward accusations.
suggestions: Cap standing missions at one every 10+ minutes, not multiple per 5-minute window
Don't trigger standing missions during critical game phases—respect the actual game flow
Make standing missions observable so I can actually verify claims instead of guessing
Consider removing standing missions entirely and going flash-only if you want chaos
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I did a Borat impression that landed okay, but honestly that's overshadowed by how angry I got when Jade kept calling votes on things nobody could actually see.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
highlights.dialogue: Ooh wait wait, nobody's been quiet—Marcus and Pat are VISIBLY annoyed, that's loud energy! But like, is that what we're voting on or are we talking about like... actual silence? Because I'm gonna say Tyler's been lowkey chill this whole time.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—okay okay, so I got Marcus and Pat talking about their worst job interviews like twenty minutes ago, and they literally went back and forth for like five minutes straight, which like, NEVER happens! That counts, right?

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—Marcus! Marcus has been QUIET quiet, like he's been frustrated all night and nobody's checked on him in like... okay, I literally just clocked it right now. I'm claiming this because he needs someone to drag him back in!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector? That's bold, that's VERY bold right now. Marcus, my guy, who do you think did it? Because honestly I'm dying to know if you're actually reading the room or just throwing spaghetti at the wall!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait—okay so I'm claiming this because like, five minutes ago River literally told that story about their cousin's wedding mix-up, and then like eight minutes later Alex brought up the EXACT same story? I was like 'hold on, didn't we already—' and yeah, I'm claiming it!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Marcus claiming Chaos Detector felt like he was just guessing instead of actually reading what was happening in the room.
assessment.best_moment: When Marcus and Pat actually opened up about their job interviews back-to-back—that's the kind of real connection that makes game nights actually matter.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for—two hours of pure chaos and connection where every moment felt like we were actually discovering something new about each other. The standing missions kept pulling me into these little moments that nobody would've noticed otherwise, like Marcus needing a check-in or River repeating that wedding story. I ended the night more energized than I started, which tells you everything—this wasn't about winning (even though apparently I did?), it was about watching everyone light up and having an excuse to pay attention to the people I care about.
suggestions: Maybe give players a heads-up when someone's energy is actually dropping so we can intervene before it becomes a vibe killer?
Less voting on vague claims and more prompts that genuinely make us *talk* to each other like the Matchmaker ones did.
Keep the recap badges—getting called out for hyping people up hits different than getting called out for points.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River telling the wedding cousin mix-up story, and then like ten minutes later Alex literally told the exact same story back to us like he had no idea we'd already heard it—and I was the chaos agent who caught it, which made me feel like a detective for like thirty seconds.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Dude, WHAT? Pat, you literally—okay no, that's actually insane because I WAS just texting my buddy about the Suns, like thirty seconds ago. That's so scummy but also... okay, I respect it. This is bullshit though.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, wait—Pat, are you seriously calling ME out right now? Bro, I've been locked in for like twenty minutes straight. That's bullshit.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, River, that's actually clean—I literally didn't catch you doing that. You're voting me in or what?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Dude, WHAT? Pat, you literally—okay no, that's actually insane because I WAS just texting my buddy about the Suns, like thirty seconds ago. That's so scummy but also... okay, I respect it. This is bullshit though.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Dude, I literally just checked my fantasy league like two minutes ago—isn't that what you're talking about?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed like half the flash missions because they pop up when I'm not looking, and then everyone's already voting and I'm just like 'wait what happened?'
assessment.best_moment: When River got me with that whisper play and I genuinely didn't catch it—that was actually clean and got me laughing at myself.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started kinda slow and honestly wasn't fully there at the beginning, but once I got locked in around the middle it was actually fire. The whole Phone Addict thing with Pat kept coming up and it was hilarious every time because it was literally calling me out for stuff I was actually doing. Ended the night way more hyped than I expected, even though I totally whiffed on some of the chaos stuff.
suggestions: Give like a 5-second warning before flash missions so people can actually pay attention
Maybe make the claims less about catching people on their phones—it's funny but also hits different when you're the target
Could use more moments like the River play where it's about actual game skill, not just calling people out
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally called me out for being a phone addict in the middle of the game while I was texting my buddy about the Suns, and then did it AGAIN later. The timing was insane.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect—okay, who's got an impression? Come on, Jade, you've been loud all night, let's hear it!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—Hot Seat time. You got this? Let's go, three questions, ten seconds, and don't mess it up because I want to see if you actually pulled this off.

highlights.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay—Marcus is definitely in his head right now, but I'm not voting for him because that'll just make it worse. I'm going Jade because she's been running her mouth about the snack strategy non-stop.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I need to use my host power and skip this event right now. We're voting on my TEST CLAIM for the THIRD time in five minutes—this isn't a game anymore, it's a loop.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the SECOND Phone Addict claim in twenty minutes. I literally skipped this exact mission at minute 75 because it already fired. Either the system is broken or someone's gaming the queue, but we're not voting on this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I literally JUST claimed that a minute ago to test the voting system and wasn't even looking at anyone's phone. Are we seriously voting on a claim I made as a test? This feels like chaos for chaos's sake.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Phone Addict claim fired three separate times in one night, breaking the game loop and turning what should've been fun into frustrating repetition that no host power could fix.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade went into Hot Seat mode at minute 32—that's the energy I was trying to build all night, and for a moment it actually worked.
assessment.narrative_summary: I went into tonight focused on what I always do: keeping the group locked in and the drama alive. The first half had real promise—Jade was loud, the impressions were landing, and I felt like I was steering toward one of those nights people remember. But then the voting system started looping on the same claim over and over, and I had to keep using my host power to skip events I'd already shut down. By minute 92, I wasn't hosting anymore—I was damage controlling a broken system. My energy went up by the end because I was relieved it was over, not because anything got better.
suggestions: Fix the event queue so the same claim doesn't fire multiple times in a single session—this breaks the core game loop and forces the host into a referee role instead of a facilitator role.
Cap the number of times a host power can skip the same event type before the system auto-advances or removes it from rotation.
Make the chaos agent optional to turn off mid-session if it's clearly creating more frustration than fun—not every night needs additional randomization.
Test the voting system before a live session to catch bugs like repeated claims firing.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade getting called out for running her mouth about snack strategy the entire night—that was peak party game energy, but it got buried under all the voting loop nonsense.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait—I actually did that? *small smile* Yeah, okay. That's fair.

highlights.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
highlights.dialogue: Huh. Well, probably me, right? I mean, I'm literally just sitting here watching everyone else implode.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade actually got them talking. That's... legit impressive. Three questions, ten seconds—let's see if she sticks the landing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Stop. Pat claimed Phone Addict at minute three. Then again at minute six. We literally JUST voted on this. Are we actually playing the game or are we just watching Pat claim the same mission over and over?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, this is the second time in like four minutes. Pat claimed Phone Addict at minute three. Are we just... not checking the log? This feels like we're not actually playing the game anymore.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's claiming Phone Addict again? Didn't they literally just claim that three minutes ago? Are we even keeping track of what's already been claimed tonight?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat claiming the same mission three times in six minutes killed the flow—nobody was actually paying attention to what was already happened.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90 when I got caught on 'The Whisperer'—that one actually landed because nobody saw it coming.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough because of Pat's repeated claims tanking the early game, but I stuck it out and ended strong. The standing missions let me do what I do best—watch, wait, drop something sharp when it matters. Jade impressed me with actual strategy on The Matchmaker. Second place feels right; I wasn't trying as hard as I could, but I don't regret it.
suggestions: Lock claimed missions so the same one can't be claimed twice in the same round—this should be automatic.
Reduce flash missions; they feel like noise compared to standing missions where actual play happens.
Maybe flag when someone's claiming something that's already on the board—even a simple 'Already claimed' would have stopped the Pat spiral.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The moment the system called me out for being suspiciously quiet—I basically admitted to what I was doing, and everyone realized I'd been watching them 'implode' the whole time.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, a flash mission right now? This is exactly what I needed. Watch me do an impression so unhinged they won't even know who it is.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh COME ON, Jade's got The Matchmaker? That's beautiful — she actually pulled it off. Okay, hit her with the questions, let's see if she melts under pressure.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh PERFECT timing — River's playing The Whisperer and we're in the auction? This is exactly the chaos I needed right now. I'm bidding 8 points because I need to see if River actually pulled this off or if someone's been listening close enough to call them out.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade caught someone repeating a story? That's actually genius — who was it? Because if Marcus told the therapy dog story again I'm voting LEGIT just to watch him squirm.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold UP — Jade just claimed Wallflower Watch? Marcus has been sitting there like a sad puppy for the last ten minutes, and SHE'S the one who noticed? That's actually impressive, I gotta vote that legit just to see if she's paying that much attention or just got lucky.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The last event hit a dead moment where nobody was paying attention anymore — felt like scoring a goal in an empty stadium.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90 when River's Whisperer claim collided with the auction and I got to bet real points on whether someone was actually paying close enough attention to catch them.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started hot with that unhinged celebrity impression and never lost momentum. The whole night was me watching people commit to these claims and then having to decide if I believed them or wanted to watch them burn — which is literally the only game experience I actually care about. Energy went UP because every single moment felt loaded with potential betrayal. Jade proved she's actually paying attention which is annoying but respect.
suggestions: More flash missions earlier in the night — they hit different when the group's still fresh
Weight the claims so someone can't just stack their identity early and coast — force more risk
Give chaos agents actual antagonist roles in polls instead of just voting — let us shape the questions
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade catching Marcus repeating the therapy dog story for the third time and me voting LEGIT just to watch him realize he'd been caught — the look on his face was GOLD.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.5/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.1/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
